name: Vairochana
shortDescription: Vairochana is a celestial Buddha often considered the embodiment of Dharmakaya, the body of truth. He represents the all-encompassing wisdom of the Buddhas and is central to many esoteric Buddhist traditions.
description: Vairochana Buddha is a central figure in Mahayana and Vajrayana Buddhism, particularly within East Asian and Tibetan Buddhist schools. He is often regarded as the primordial or first Dhyani Buddha, representing the Dharmakaya, the body of truth, which is the ultimate reality and the source of all phenomena. Vairochana embodies the wisdom of the Dharmadhatu, the sphere of reality as it truly is, free from limitations and dualistic thinking. He is often depicted as white or golden in color and is associated with the element of ether or space. His mudra, the gesture of turning the wheel of Dharma, symbolizes the teaching and propagation of Buddhist principles throughout the universe. Vairochana's position within the mandala reflects his central role as the source of all Buddhas and Bodhisattvas. Understanding Vairochana is crucial for grasping the concept of emptiness (sunyata) and the interconnectedness of all things, foundational aspects of Mahayana philosophy. He is a manifestation of prajna (transcendent wisdom) and is often associated with learning, scholarship, eloquence, teaching, memory, and intelligence, reflecting the importance of understanding emptiness for achieving enlightenment. His association with Manjushri further reinforces the connection to wisdom and skillful means in Buddhist practice. In some traditions, Vairochana is considered the central figure of the Five Dhyani Buddhas, each representing a different aspect of enlightened consciousness and a corresponding element. He is not simply a deity to be worshipped but a representation of the potential for enlightenment within each individual.

sources.title: The Encyclopedia of Eastern Philosophy and Religion
sources.author: Ingrid Fischer-Schreiber, Franz-Karl Ehrhard, Michael S. Diener
